clinical_history: This ECG from a 62 year old woman with a history of atrial fibrillation is most consistent with which ONE of the following rhythms?
diagnosis: The ECG shows a regular wide complex tachycardia at about 150/min. In strong favor of ventricular tachycardia are two major findings: the broad RS waves in V1 and V2 with an atypical RBBB morphology and the very wide QRS duration (180 msec). No atrial activity is apparent. The patient had a history of mitral valve replacement and prior inferior-lateral MI with left ventricular (LV) dilation and a reduced LV ejection fraction of 35-40~. This rhythm required DC cardioversion and the patient underwent successful radiofrequency catheter ablation of VT sites in the LV at the inferior wall to mitral valve regions.
